description_long: This is a historic stone watermill building with a large wooden water wheel attached to one side. The structure is primarily constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, giving it a robust and weathered appearance, and features a gabled roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The water wheel, made of wood, is mounted on the side and is supported by a stone-lined mill race that channels water to power the wheel. The building has multiple rectangular windows with simple frames, and two large arched doorways at the base, likely for access to the mill's interior and machinery. Overhead, utility poles and wires are visible, suggesting some degree of modernization or adaptation for contemporary use. The overall design is utilitarian yet picturesque, typical of rural or semi-urban mills found near rivers or streams.
description: A robust stone watermill with a large wooden water wheel, gabled tile roof, and multiple rectangular windows and arched doorways.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stone facade, two large arched doorways, several rectangular windows, and the attached wooden water wheel.
description_view_1: End view focused on the water wheel, gabled roof, and a central window above the wheel, with the mill race visible below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, but with the water wheel less visible and utility poles more prominent.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the gabled roof, central window, and stone wall, with no water wheel visible.
attribute_water_wheel: Large overshot wooden water wheel, approximately 3.5 meters in diameter, constructed from timber with 16 visible paddles, prominent radial spokes, and a central hub; shows weathering and robust build.
attribute_mill_race: Stone-lined open channel, visible on the side of the building, approximately 1.5 meters wide and 6 meters long, directing water to the base of the wheel.
attribute_building_structure: Rectangular, three-story structure built primarily from rough-hewn stone blocks with a stone foundation; features arched doorways and simple rectangular windows.
attribute_roof_type: Gabled roof covered with reddish-brown tiles, no visible chimneys or dormers, moderate overhang at the eaves.
attribute_window_style: Rectangular windows with multi-pane configuration, simple wooden frames, no visible shutters or decorative elements.
